title: West Bengal polls: Bhowanipore feels the heat as Mamata and Suvendu take on each other
content: Bhabanipur witnessed high-voltage drama as Mamata Banerjee and Suvendu Adhikari traded barbs amid polling. Mamata accused the BJP of rigging, while Suvendu dismissed her claims as frustration. Tensions flared at a polling booth, requiring intervention by security forces. This phase is crucial for both TMC's stronghold and BJP's challenge in south Bengal.

title: 'Better get smart soon!' Trump tells Iran to 'get their act together'; shares AI image with a gun
content: President Trump criticized Iran's negotiation tactics, stating they "don't know how to sign a non-nuclear deal" and urged them to "get smart soon." This comes as the US reportedly prepares for an extended blockade strategy to limit Iran's regional influence, shifting from short-term measures to sustained economic and military pressure.

title: ‘AI chip in bat’? Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks silence with cheeky reply – Watch
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi humorously dismissed 'AI chip' allegations after a record-breaking IPL 2026 performance.  He cheekily attributed his success to divine intervention.  Sooryavanshi also became the fastest to 400 runs this season, showcasing his extraordinary talent and fearless batting.

title: UAE’s exit from OPEC & OPEC+: Shaking world oil order but benefit for India
content: While the world order evolves, and UAE’s decision impacts other major oil producers around the world, India which imports around 90% of its crude needs may actually stand to benefit.

title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: Why no Indian broadcaster wants the 2026 FIFA World Cup
content: With less than two months to go for the FIFA World Cup, India, China, and several Asian countries still lack an official broadcaster. High rights costs, inconvenient match timings, and cricket's dominance have deterred buyers in India. This poses challenges for FIFA’s outreach in the region, though a last-minute deal remains possible.

title: 'Historic hat-trick of victories': PM Modi hails 'fearless voting in Bengal'
content: Prime Minister Modi lauded West Bengal's high voter turnout in the second phase of assembly elections, highlighting a "fearless atmosphere." He expressed confidence in a BJP hat-trick of victories across five states, citing recent successes in Bihar and Gujarat. The results on May 4 are anticipated to bolster the resolve for a developed India.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: IPL 2026: Yashasvi Jaiswal blown away by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi show - ‘I don’t know what to say…’
content: Rajasthan Royals secured a thrilling six-wicket victory over Punjab Kings, powered by a sensational opening partnership between Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Jaiswal lauded Sooryavanshi's aggressive batting, emphasizing their clear intent to chase the massive total.  Despite a mid-innings wobble, the team's strong environment and belief ultimately led them to a memorable win.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection: Ranveer Singh starrer eyes shattering 'Baahubali 2' record
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi, Sara Arjun and Yami Gautam in a cameo, is just Rs. 7.24 crore away from surpassing 'Baahubali 2: The Conclusion's nine-year worldwide record of Rs. 1,788.06 crore to become the second highest-grossing Indian film of all time globally after 'Dangal'. On Day 42, the film's worldwide gross stands at Rs. 1,780.82 crore with an India net of Rs. 1,133.05 crore, approximately Rs. 101 crore away from 'Pushpa 2: The Rule's all-time domestic record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore. Released across 10,000 screens on March 19, 2026 with a record Rs. 90 crore opening day, the 229-minute U/A certified espionage thriller has achieved all of this without a single show in China or the six Gulf markets where it remains banned.

title: US to issue special passports for America's 250th birthday: Here's what will be new
content: The US State Department is releasing a limited batch of commemorative passports for America's 250th birthday, featuring Donald Trump's image and signature. These special passports, available soon at the Washington D.C. office, will have design changes but retain security features. Standard passports will continue to be issued elsewhere.

title: Keymer Exclusive: Being German No. 1, Magnus intimidation, and more
content: In this exclusive interview, Vincent Keymer reflected on his rise from winning the Grenke Open at 13 to claiming the Grenke Freestyle 2026 title in Karlsruhe. Germany’s No. 1 spoke about freestyle chess relying more on intuition, handling the “intimidation factor” of Magnus Carlsen, and balancing school with chess while growing up in a family of musicians. Keymer also praised coach Péter Lékó and discussed the challenges young chess players face in Germany compared to countries like India.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the cheat code batter - Even Bumrah & Co. have nowhere to hide
content: At just fifteen, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is setting the IPL 2026 alight with his breathtaking batting skills, reminiscent of legends like Sachin Tendulkar. His audacious style against elite bowlers, boasting strike rates often soaring past 200, challenges every cricketing norm. In Sooryavanshi’s case, he has aced that test like no other. Almost like using the cheat codes in EA Sports Cricket 07.

title: PM Modi inaugurates UP's longest expressway; check travel time, route, speed limit
content: One of the most noticeable benefits of Ganga Expressway will be the sharp reduction in travel time. The trip between Meerut and Prayagraj, which currently takes around 10 to 12 hours, is likely to be cut to approximately 6 to 7 hours.

title: Why a gunman at the WHCD epitomises American idiosyncrasy
content: Jean Paul-Sartre once said: Freedom is what you do with what’s been done to you, and America – with its freedom from Britain and other colonial powers – develop its own peculiarities, one of which is the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, a night where the press and the White House ostensibly bury their differences over a few shots. Normally those shots are jokes like Obama’s anger translator or Reagan dialling in after an assassination attempt, but this year they came from the barrel of a deranged gunman who had managed to waltz past security.

title: Babar breaks massive T20 record; joins Kohli, Abhishek in elite list
content: Babar Azam's spectacular century propelled Peshawar Zalmi to the PSL final, defeating Islamabad United. His unbeaten 103 powered Zalmi to 221, a total too daunting for United. This remarkable innings marked Babar's fourth PSL century, equalling a record, and his ninth T20 hundred as captain, a new global benchmark.

title: Ghaziabad family’s ‘destination wedding’ at Almora cremation ground sparks massive row
content: A riverside cremation site in Uttarakhand's Almora district was used for a destination wedding, sparking outrage and prompting an administration probe. The ceremony, which involved a couple from Ghaziabad and around 50 guests, took place on April 25 at a ghat traditionally used for last rites.

title: Mass resignations rock Sri Lanka Cricket as prez, executive committee quit
content: Shammi Silva resigned as president of Sri Lanka Cricket along with the board’s entire executive committee, the organisation confirmed on Wednesday. Silva had led SLC since 2019, overseeing Asia Cup successes but also disappointing World Cup campaigns and declining rankings for the men’s team. The reason for the mass resignations has not been officially disclosed.

title: West Bengal polls: Burdwan homes go without help as maid gets BJP ticket
content: In a devastating turn of events, two cousins aged 20 and 14 were killed in a hit-and-run accident on Ashoka Road in central Delhi, just after enjoying an IPL match. The massive truck, which turned recklessly at the Mahadev Road T-point, crushed the young duo on their motorbike.

title: From Riyan Parag to ABD: Cricketers seen vaping on camera
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag is in hot water after visuals of him vaping in the dressing room during an IPL 2026 match went viral. This incident raises serious concerns as vaping is banned in India. It adds to a string of player conduct controversies in the league.  The league faces renewed scrutiny over player behavior and rule enforcement.

title: Riddhi Dogra recalls suffering from ‘anxiety’ while playing Shah Rukh Khan’s mother in Jawan
content: Riddhi Dogra, a recent fan-favorite from 'The 50', shared her surreal experience playing Shah Rukh Khan's mother in 'Jawan'. She revealed her childhood admiration for SRK fueled her decision, despite initial anxieties about portraying an older role. Director Atlee's team struggled to age her, ultimately embracing her youthful appearance as a deliberate narrative choice for the film's flashback sequences.

title: Sonam Raghuvanshi gets bail in 4th attempt over 'pre-arrest lapses'; what happened during court hearing
content: The recent bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi by a local court has left many in disbelief, especially in light of accusations that she planned her husband’s murder during their honeymoon. The decision, hinged on perceived missteps in the arrest notification, has led to public outcry from the victim's family.

title: 'No vacuum in law to deal with the offence' — What Supreme Court said on hate speeches
content: The Supreme Court ruled that current laws adequately address hate speech, stating punishment is a legislative matter and no vacuum exists. However, it encouraged the government to assess if new legislation is needed due to changing social dynamics.

title: Watch: 108 Ambulance soiled by accident victim, staffer takes money, tells wife to clean it
content: A 108 ambulance staffer in Katni district allegedly extorted money from a critically injured road accident victim's family and forced a female relative to clean the ambulance. The disturbing incident, captured on video, has led to an official inquiry and a notice issued to the staffer for an explanation.

title: 'Same aura': 15-year-old Sooryavanshi compared to Dhoni, Tendulkar
content: At just 15, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has become one of the IPL’s biggest attractions with his fearless batting and powerful strokeplay. Former India pacer Mohit Sharma compared the youngster’s nationwide popularity and “aura” to MS Dhoni, saying he has seen only two players command such admiration. Ex-India cricketer Murali Karthik also hailed Sooryavanshi as a “genuine prodigy”, comparing his rise and fame to that of Sachin Tendulkar.

title: Gujarat local body election: Hindu woman scripts victory in Muslim-majority Godhra ward
content: In a remarkable display of unity, independent candidate Apexa Soni secured a win in Godhra's communally sensitive Ward 7, a locality with minimal Hindu voters. Residents, primarily from the Muslim community, rallied behind Soni, who had consistently addressed local civic issues even after a narrow loss in 2021. Her victory highlights people transcending divisions for a dedicated public servant.

title: ‘It’s an arduous task': Iyer on growing crisis for bowlers after loss
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer lamented bowling execution after their first IPL 2026 loss. Despite a strong 224, the team couldn't contain Rajasthan Royals' late surge, particularly from Ferreira and Dubey. Iyer acknowledged the challenge for bowlers in modern T20s but stressed execution is key. He remains optimistic, calling it a learning experience.

title: Ghaziabad apartment fire: Massive blaze erupts in Indirapuram, multiple flats gutted
content: A massive fire erupted at Gaur Green Avenue society in Indirapuram on Wednesday morning, gutting an estimated 10 to 12 flats and causing panic among residents. Fire tenders responded swiftly to the blaze, which is suspected to have been triggered by an electrical fault. Dousing operations are underway to prevent further spread.

title: Is Trump getting the full picture? Vance raises concerns over Iran war
content: US Vice President J D Vance is questioning the Pentagon's assessment of the Iran conflict, concerned about undisclosed missile stockpile depletion. He fears this could impact responses to global flashpoints like Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe.  Despite official assurances, some believe the situation is being downplayed, potentially affecting future military readiness.

title: Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: Lizard in paneer tikka? Bengaluru cabbie hospitalised after bakery visit
content: A man in Bengaluru was hospitalized after allegedly finding a lizard in his paneer tikka roll from a local bakery. He experienced vomiting and diarrhea, leading to a 24-hour observation period. Police have registered a case, though the complainant could not provide photographic evidence of the alleged contamination.

title: IPL 2026: For batters, struggle is real in slightly tough conditions
content: Having Been Spoon-Fed T20 Belters, They Have Been Unable To Adapt When Needed Most. Should Indian Cricket Be Worried About Success Away From Home?

title: Bengal phase 2 polls: TMC worker attacked, BJP claims voter booth vandalised
content: West Bengal's second phase of Assembly elections commenced amidst reports of violence, voter intimidation, and technical issues. Incidents were reported across Chapra, Shantipur, Nimtala, and Bhangar. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged bias from observers and TMC party flags being removed, while the BJP reported an assault on a polling agent in Chapra.

title: CPI(M) to launch protests from May 3 over delay in resolving ‘Pancha Gramalu’ land issue
content: Party district secretary M. Jaggunaidu says Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu had promised in his first Cabinet meeting to resolve the Panchagramala land issue within 100 days; alleges that two years on, no action has been take

title: West Bengal election Phase 2: Mamata, Suvendu land in same booth area in Bhabanipur
content: TMC chief Mamata also raised the issue of alleged unrest in Bhangar and accused the Election Commission of India of failing to ensure neutrality

title: Bengaluru city police launch India’s first multilingual AI system in Namma 112 emergency service
content: The system currently supports more than 10 languages — Indian languages such as Bengali, Gujarati, Odia, Manipuri, Kashmiri, Nepali and Assamese, along with international languages like Spanish, French and Arabic.

title: Parallel Central force structure in Bengal as officials are under pressure, says Akhilesh Yadav
content: The Samajwadi Party president further claimed that even at the level of the Director General of Police (DGP), there were indications of interference

title: Law permits Perarivalan to practise as lawyer: T.N. Bar Council enrolment committee chairman
content: K. Balu says Section 24A of the Advocates Act permits former convicts to enrol as lawyers after a cooling-off period of two years pursuant to their release from prison

title: Election Commission completes post-poll scrutiny in Tamil Nadu, West Bengal; no re-polls ordered
content: The verification exercise was conducted on April 24 across all 152 Assembly constituencies in West Bengal and 234 constituencies in Tamil Nadu, in the presence of general observers and candidates or their representatives

title: Banda sizzles at 45.6℃ as heatwave grips Uttar Pradesh
content: The IMD has forecast mainly clear to partly cloudy skies over Lucknow and adjoining areas, with the possibility of one or two spells of rain or thunderstorms

title: Four proposals to link Tummidihatti with Yellampalli under study: Telangana Irrigation Minister Uttam
content: Congress government reiterates commitment to Pranahita-Chevella project. Restoration of barrages ordered ahead of monsoon

title: West Bengal Assembly polls Phase 2 LIVE: State records 78.68% voter turnout till 3 p.m. amid sporadic violence
content: According to the Election Commission of India (ECI), Purba Bardhaman records 83.11%, followed by Hooghly at 80.77%, and Howrah at 77.73% at 3 p.m.

title: Bombay HC modifies order after adjourning defamation suit to 2046; posts hearing in July
content: The HC had noted that this was one of the matters where the "ego fight" between the parties at the fag end of their lives "clogs the system", preventing the court from taking up cases which require priority

title: Contract employee arrested for downloading, watching leaked ‘Jana Nayagan’ at Chennai’s vote-counting centre
content: During poll duty hours, the employee – working in the CCTV room – allegedly downloaded and watched the unreleased film illegally on his laptop, took a photograph of it, and posted it on his Facebook account around 11 p.m. on April 23

title: Congress MP writes to President objecting to bar enrolment of Perarivalan
content: R. Sudha says individuals convicted of serious offences, such as acts of terrorism, cannot be allowed to practise as lawyers

title: Great Nicobar project one of biggest scams, gravest crimes against natural, tribal heritage: Rahul
content: Stating that the project would entail the axing of millions of trees on 160 sq km of rainforest, and calling it a "destruction dressed in development's language", the leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha said he would raise the issue in Parliament

title: Ambadas Danve to be Shiv Sena (UBT)'s candidate for Maharashtra council election
content: The announcement by party MLA Aaditya Thackeray ruled out the possibility that Uddhav Thackeray would be the opposition's candidate

title: Kerala pollution control board prepares draft oil spill response plan
content: A Bengaluru-based firm has submitted the draft plan that includes oil spill modelling, marine sensitivity index mapping and net environmental benefit analysis, according to a report submitted by KSPCB before NGT

title: Railway worker booked on charge of raping woman passenger on Visakha Express
content: Accused, engaged in supplying bedsheets in AC coaches, allegedly approached woman and her husband seated near a toilet in a general compartment and persuaded them to move to an AC coach, citing better seating

title: Andhra Pradesh swaps senior planners between APCRDA and VMRDA on deputation
content: K. Divya Latha, Joint Director (Planning) at APCRDA, posted as Chief Urban Planner at VMRDA on deputation; V. Shilpa, Chief Urban Planner at VMRDA, sent to APCRDA as Joint Director

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Sholinganallur constituency registers a massive jump in voter turnout
content: While the constituency registered a turnout of 55% in 2021, this year, the provisional figures released by election authorities stood at around 80.4%

title: PRADA to provide skill training to Athani leather artisans
content: This is part of an arrangement by the luxury fashion brand with Karnataka and Maharashtra to help conserve the Kolhapuri Chappal heritage

title: PM Modi inaugurates 594-km Ganga Expressway connecting 12 districts from Meerut to Prayagraj
content: CM Adityanath described the six-lane (expandable to eight) expressway as a "lifeline" connecting villages, farmers, entrepreneurs and youth

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Absolute voter increases seen across State followed spatial patterns
content: Data show the record turnout in the southern State was a consequence of the SIR exercise; while the State also registered an increase in the absolute number of voters who turned out, the provisional addition of roughly 24.8 lakh voters appears lower than the gains registered in recent cycles

title: The Hindu launches tabloid ‘TeenDigest’ for young readers
content: A revamp of The Hindu In School, the 32-page edition weekend tabloid aims to engage curious young minds in an age of endless scrolling. Content ranges from deep dives into science and world affairs, to stories designed to spark creativity

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Absolute voter increases seen across State followed spatial patterns
content: While the SIR’s deletions muted turnout in cities like Chennai, northern and semi-urban areas of Tamil nadu registered a good increase in voters compared to 2021 election

title: Forest Department steps up summer measures for wildlife in Erode
content: Drinking water troughs have been installed at strategic locations and are being filled using solar-powered motor systems

title: Kerala Film Critics Awards: Mystery thriller Eko wins Best Film
content: Dominic Arun bags best director award for Lokah Chapter 1: Chandra. Mohanlal adjudged best actor (male) for  Thudarum and Hridayapoorvam. Kalyani Priyadarshan (Lokah) and Anaswara Rajan (Rekhachithram, Mr and Mrs Bachelor) share best actor (female) award

title: Amid power crisis, Kerala govt directs KSEB to take control of Maniyar hydro project
content: Kerala government’s decision comes more than a year after 30-year contract period with Carborundum Universal ended. The 12-MW captive power plant was at the centre of a political controversy earlier after State govt hesitated to allow KSEB to take over the project

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Polling percentage of women rises in State
content: As per provisional figures available at 8 p.m. on Friday, two Assembly constituencies in Tamil Nadu — Karur and Veerapandi — posted 93.40% each, the highest polling percentage among the Assembly segments that went to polls on April 23

title: Delhi Excise policy case: HC adjourns to May 4 CBI plea against Kejriwal’s discharge
content: AAP leaders Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia and Durgesh Pathak have written to Delhi HC Justice Swarana Kantha Sharma, saying that they will not appear in person or through a lawyer before the Court

title: SC/ST Commission recommends action against officials for non-removal of caste from ward’s name in Srivilliputhur constituency
content: Residents of the area boycotted the election to protest the government’s failure to remove the caste name

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Congress leaders introspect on not aligning with TVK
content: Many have said Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, who did not share the stage with Chief Minister M.K. Stalin and was widely believed to have favoured an alliance with the TVK, had assessed the situation accurately when he saw an opportunity to rejuvenate the Congress in Tamil Nadu

title: IPS officer gets PhD from Kuvempu University for his thesis on wildlife
content: Harsha P.S., Inspector-General of Police, conducted the research under the guidance of Vijay Kumar of the Department of Wildlife and Management

title: Jharkhand Police arrest prime accused in West Singhbhum treasury-fraud case
content: In the wake of the scam, Jharkhand Finance Minister Radha Krishna Kishore had ordered a comprehensive audit of all district treasuries

title: Three policeman suspended after student found dead in Dharwad, mother alleges extortion
content: As allegations of police harassment emerged after a student was found dead in Dharwad, three policemen were suspended on Wednesday

title: Nandyal Collector, SP launch month-long drive to fix 29 black spots on highways
content: District recorded 567 road accidents and 296 deaths in 2025, up from 542 accidents and 271 deaths in 2024; nine of the 29 identified black spots on NH-40, seven on NH-44 and six within Nandyal town

title: Man gets 20-year imprisonment for sexually assaulting minor girl in Namakkal
content: The accused, Vasanth, was arrested based on a complaint lodged by the girl’s parents with the Namagiripettai police on December 29, 2019

title: In 2026, Tamil Nadu records lowest increase in votes polled during Assembly elections over the last 15 years
content: While this election saw an all-time high turnout of 85.15%, a look at the data in the elections held since 2011 shows that there is no uniform pattern between the turnout and electoral verdict

title: One of the two tiger cubs rescued in Nilgiris dies
content: Two tiger cubs were taken into captivity, one after the other, after they were believed to have been abandoned by their mother

title: Three-tier security in place at counting centres in central T.N.
content: EVMs from over 11,000 polling stations in central region were taken to the respective counting centres amid tight security after the polling on April 23

title: Green NGO ‘Parisarakkaagi Naavu’ writes to PM demanding immediate action to protect people from climate emergencies
content: The organisation has demanded that the felling of any tree on public land, in catchment areas and urban areas, and on notified forest land must be made a cognisable, non-bailable offence, with mandatory minimum penalties

title: Karnataka HC refuses to quash case against man for filming voyeuristic videos of women on Bengaluru Metro and posting on social media
content: “What kind of man are you? You were taking photos of women from the backside and posting them on Instagram... You don’t even let women be safe anywhere,” the court observed orally.

title: Chhattisgarh Police team detained in Guwahati over alleged bribery case
content: Police said an FIR was registered under bailable sections, after which the team was allowed to leave the police station on notice following preliminary interrogation

title: West Bengal polls: PM Modi must resign when TMC wins, Derek O'Brien says
content: The remarks come amidst an intensifying political contest in West Bengal, where the ruling TMC and the Bharatiya Janata Party are engaged in a high-stakes electoral battle

title: U.S. returns 657 stolen artefacts to India in $14 million restitution
content: Sources said the 657 antiquities were delivered in three phases: 612 were returned in November 2024, 26 in July 2025, and in the third phase, 19 were returned to India on Tuesday (April 28, 2026).

title: Forest fire near Yercaud doused after two-hour operation
content: No casualties were reported. However, several trees, herbs, plants and creepers were destroyed in the fire.

title: NSUI activists ransack V-C secretariat in Kuvempu University at Shivamogga during protest over failure to declare results
content: They also alleged that the university had not rectified discrepancies in the results despite repeated appeals

title: Yash’s ‘Toxic’ postponed again: We are aligning global distribution, say makers
content: ‘Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups, the much-anticipated film starring Yash and directed by Geetu Mohandas, has been pushed back indefinitely. The movie was set to hit the theatres on June 04, 2026

title: Dhar State court declared Bhojshala a ‘mosque’ in 1935, Muslim side claims in Madhya Pradesh High Court
content: The Hindu community considers Bhojshala, located in Dhar district, a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, while the Muslim side claims it to be the Kamal Maula Mosque

title: Global giant Heineken’s GCC in Hyderabad inaugurated
content: It is expected to create over 300 high-value jobs in the initial phase, with employment projected to scale up to 1,600 in the coming years, says Telangana IT and Industries Minister’s office.

title: Siberian Huskies abandoned on Hyderabad outskirts, found severely dehydrated; one dead, eight traced
content: The dogs, aged between 2-3 years, were left in open areas far from habitation, with no access to shade or water during peak summer conditions

title: No rest for the wicked: Even as Tamil Nadu comes down from its election high, tensions are still rising
content: The issues vary from the advisability of Chief Minister M.K.Stalin taking a holiday in Kodaikkanal to the comparative performance of Tamil Nadu with other southern States with respect to the IT sector to the possibility of a coalition regime getting formed due to the presence of the Vijay-led TVK

title: Ontavantha Pidariyum Oor Pidariyum: The story behind the proverb
content: The proverb’s origins are traced to Udayalur in Thanjavur district, according to noted epigraphist T.N. Subramanian

title: Bengal phase 2 poll heats up: Mamata's 'rigging' charge evokes Suvendu's 'creating fear' retort
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: Jagan Reddy's Party Alleges Fuel Shortage In Andhra, Writes To Centre
content: YSRCP MP Maddila Gurumoorthy in a letter to Union Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri sought an urgent intervention in Andhra Pradesh

title: Opinion: Opinion | There's A Bigger Plan Behind UAE's Exit From OPEC - One In Force For Years Now
content: Surprising as the UAE's decision is, it is also fully in keeping with its pattern of breaking with regional, in particular, Arab consensus on many issues and forging an independent path.

title: Bengal Elections Phase 2 Voting LIVE: Bengal Records Over 78% Voter Turnout Till 3 Pm
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 2 Voting LIVE Updates: The second phase of voting for the intense BJP vs Trinamool battle will begin shortly.

content: The looming storage crunch is threatening to accelerate production cuts in what was once the second-largest source of crude under the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC). P

title: "You Need Rs 40 Crore To Retire": Founder's Claim Sparks Online Debate
content: The viral clip went viral as it exposed the gap between what people assume retirement needs and what long-term inflation can do to expenses.

title: Ukraine's New Card In War Strategy: Attacks On Russian Oil Infrastructure
content: The refinery, which lies 900 kilometres southeast of Kyiv and delivers oil products mainly for exports, has annual production capacity of about 12 million metric tons.

title: 'Let Lawmakers Act': Supreme Court Ends Tryst With Hate Speech Cases
content: The notion that the offence of hate speech is not covered under the legal framework is misconceived, the court observed.

title: Dr Reddy's Wins Nod to Sell Generic Ozempic, Check Cost Of Weight-Loss Drug
content: The entry of Dr Reddy's generic is triggering a price battle among pharmaceutical companies worldwide.

title: Ahmedabad Cyber Police Bust Rs 47 Lakh Insurance Scam, 2 Arrested
content: The investigation revealed a meticulously planned "modus operandi" designed to exploit the trust of unsuspecting individuals.

title: Porn Clips Play During Delhi High Court Chief Justice's Virtual Proceedings
content: A complaint has been lodged with the Delhi Police's Intelligence Fusion & Strategic Operations (IFSO) unit, which will now investigate the matter.

title: Gemini To Meta: How A Deepfake Gang Used AI To Hijack Identities In Gujarat
content: The gang is accused of executing a complex scheme where they acquired victim data and used AI platforms, such as Gemini AI and Meta AI, to generate high-quality deepfake videos featuring "eye-blink" animations.

title: Ex-South Korean President Gets Additional 7 Years In Jail Over Life Sentence
content: The conviction for obstruction of justice and other charges comes on top of a life sentence President Yoon Suk Yeol has already received on rebellion charges.

title: Polling Stopped At Few Bengal Booths After BJP Claims EVM Tampering
content: Amit Malviya, the BJP's media in-charge, has demanded repolling in all affected booths in Falta where such election frauds have occurred.

title: CBI To Probe Rs 397 Crore Contract Issued Under DMK's Senthil Balaji
content: The case originates from a complaint filed by anti-corruption NGO Arappor Iyakkam, which alleged large-scale collusion in the tender process between 2021 and 2023, leading to an estimated loss of Rs 397 crore to the state exchequer.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 'On Verge Of Stopping': Inside The Panic That Led To Indian Airlines' SOS Call
content: The Iran shock has hit every airline in the world. Yet, only three groups have written to governments to keep them flying. One of them comprises the three Indian airlines.

title: 'This is TMC’s technique': BJP alleges party symbol 'taped' over in Bengal's Falta

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
